topic: Complex budget analysis with pie charts
title: Reading a budget pie chart
instruction: Read the Schofield family budget information and answer the questions.
context: Schofield family monthly income: £3,200. Budget breakdown: Housing (rent + bills) 38% | Food 14% | Transport 9% | Childcare 16% | Clothing 4% | Entertainment 5% | Savings 8% | Other 6%.

questions.question: A pie chart shows budget as percentages. The whole pie represents what?
Which two categories together make up more than half the budget?
The family is advised to reduce 'discretionary spending'. Which category is discretionary?
How much does the family spend on housing each month? (38% of £3,200)
How much do they save per month? (8% of £3,200)
If they cut entertainment (5%) and redirect it to savings, what is their new monthly saving amount?
